title: Attacks on Imperial holdouts
content:
{{Top|leg}} {{Conjecture|legends}} {{Campaign |prev=[[Second Imperium Crisis]]''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' |conc= |next=[[Yuuzhan Vong War]][[Starships of the Galaxy (Saga Edition)|''Starships of the Galaxy'', Saga Edition]]''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]'' |image= |name=Attacks on Imperial holdouts |conflict=Post-[[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]] |begin= |end=[[25 ABY/Legends|25 ABY]] |date= |place=Various locations around [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] |result=[[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] victory |battles= |side1=New Republic |side2=Rogue [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] factions *Imperial [[Warlord/Legends|warlords]] *[[Second Imperium]] remnants |side3= |side4= |commanders1=[[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Gial Ackbar/Legends|Ackbar]] |commanders2= |commanders3= |commanders4= |forces1= |forces2= |forces3= |forces4= |casual1= |casual2= |casual3= |casual4= |civilian= }} Several '''attacks on Imperial holdouts''' were made by the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] after the end of the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. ==Prelude== {{Quote|Aren't there still some Imperial strongholds in the Core Systems?''"
"''Sure are.|[[Jaina Solo Fel|Jaina Solo]] and [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]]|''[[Young Jedi Knights: The Lost Ones]]''}} As the Galactic Civil War came to a formal end, with the [[Imperial Remnant]] losing ground and ultimately signing the [[Pellaeon–Gavrisom Treaty]] with the New Republic in [[19 ABY/Legends|19 ABY]], many remaining Imperial warlords had turned to mass piracy. On the other hand, some [[Deep Core/Legends|Deep Core]] [[replacement warlord]]s created the [[Second Imperium]] to continue the fight against the New Republic.''[[The New Essential Guide to Characters]]'' but they were [[Second Imperium Crisis|defeated]] by the New Republic and the [[New Jedi Order]] in [[23 ABY/Legends|23 ABY]]. ==The attacks== In an effort to consolidate its power and enforce security, the New Republic tracked down and eliminated rogue Imperial [[Warlord/Legends|warlords]] and pirates, focusing especially on their holdings in the Deep Core.''[[The New Jedi Order: Destiny's Way]]'' These campaigns saw the initial trial of the [[Viscount-class Star Defender|''Viscount''-class Star Defender]] in [[25 ABY/Legends|25 ABY]], when the prototype [[Viscount (Viscount-class)|''Viscount'']] engaged Imperial ships in combat. However, engagements were rare events. On his side, [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Gial Ackbar/Legends|Ackbar]] targeted remnants of the Second Imperium and liberated all remaining Imperium [[Fortress world/Legends|fortress worlds]]. ==Aftermath== The outcome of these battles was the decimation of rogue Imperial forces and the end to organized Imperial piracy. Deployment of the ''Viscount'' during those skirmishes was considered as a huge waste of resources and the development of the ''Viscount''-class program was soon stopped. The ''Viscount'' itself was later downgraded to ceremonial display.''[[The New Jedi Order: Vector Prime]]'' By the time of the [[Yuuzhan Vong War]], the New Republic had finally cleared all the Imperial warlords out of the Deep Core. Admiral Ackbar would retire from service following the defeat of those last Imperial forces, in 25 ABY. ==Appearances== *''[[The New Jedi Order: Destiny's Way]]'' {{C|Possible mention}} *[[The New Jedi Order: Destiny's Way (abridged audiobook)|''The New Jedi Order: Destiny's Way'' abridged audiobook]] ==Sources== *[[Starships of the Galaxy (Saga Edition)|''Starships of the Galaxy'', Saga Edition]] *''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]'' ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |es=Ataques a los reductos Imperiales }} {{DEFAULTSORT:Imperial holdouts, attacks on}} [[Category:Campaigns during the post-Imperial era]]
